title: HTML Beginner Specialist Assessment
description: Introductory assessment for entry-level developers covering document structure, semantic markup, forms, and accessibility basics.

questions.text: What does the abbreviation HTML stand for?
questions.explanation: HTML is the standard markup language for creating web pages.

questions.text: Which tag is used to define the root of an HTML document?
questions.explanation: The <html> element wraps all the content on the entire page.

questions.text: Which tag is used to create a hyperlink?
questions.explanation: The <a> (anchor) tag is used with the 'href' attribute to create links.

questions.text: Which element represents the most important heading on a page?
questions.explanation: <h1> is the highest level of heading; there should typically be only one per page.

questions.text: What is the correct tag for a line break?
questions.explanation: is an empty element used to produce a line break in text.

questions.text: Which attribute is used to provide an image with a text description?
questions.explanation: The 'alt' attribute is crucial for accessibility and displays if the image cannot load.

questions.text: How do you create an unordered list?
questions.explanation: <ul> stands for Unordered List, typically rendered with bullet points.

questions.text: Which HTML element is used to specify a footer for a document or section?
questions.explanation: The <footer> element is a semantic tag used for the bottom of a page or section.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the <head> element?
options: To display the main title on the page
To contain metadata and links to scripts/stylesheets
To show the header of the website
questions.explanation: The <head> contains machine-readable information that isn't shown directly to users.

questions.text: Which input type allows the user to select one or more options from a list?
questions.explanation: Checkboxes allow multiple selections; radio buttons typically allow only one.

questions.text: What is the correct HTML for adding a background color?
questions.explanation: Inline styles or CSS are used for styling, as 'bgcolor' attributes are deprecated.

questions.text: Which semantic tag is used for navigation links?
questions.explanation: The <nav> element is intended for major blocks of navigation links.

questions.text: What does the <iframe> tag do?
options: Embeds another document within the current HTML document
Creates a frame around a paragraph
questions.explanation: Iframes are used to embed content like maps, videos, or other pages.

questions.text: Which attribute must be used for an <a> tag to actually link to a URL?
questions.explanation: The 'href' (hypertext reference) attribute specifies the destination address.

questions.text: Which element is used to define a table row?
questions.explanation: <tr> stands for Table Row.

questions.text: What is the correct HTML for making a checkbox?
questions.explanation: The <input> tag with type='checkbox' creates a toggleable box.

questions.text: In HTML, which attribute is used to specify an input field is required?
questions.explanation: The 'required' attribute prevents form submission if the field is empty.

questions.text: Which tag is used to define an item in a list?
questions.explanation: <li> (List Item) is used inside <ul> or <ol> tags.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the <aside> tag?
options: To display text in the margin
To define content aside from the main content (like a sidebar)
To place content at the bottom
questions.explanation: The <aside> element represents content indirectly related to the surrounding content.

questions.text: Which tag is used to define a multi-line text input?
questions.explanation: <textarea> allows for multiple lines of user input, unlike <input>.
